package: Goal 23: Regulated Static-Patch dS/CFT Algebraic ER=EPR Testbed

scope.setting: regulated finite static-patch screen model with angular cutoff L, finite screen mode algebra C^N, observer algebras M_N, shared horizon C^N, and one geometric transfer kernel
scope.result_type: finite-cutoff screen-data no-go plus intrinsic operator-response completion
scope.claim_boundary: regulated finite testbed; not continuum dS/CFT, de Sitter quantum gravity, Type-II/Type-III observer algebra, or ER=EPR in de Sitter

core_claim: At finite cutoff, dS/CFT-like screen entropy, low-order correlator, horizon-overlap, and screen-restricted transfer data do not determine the induced algebraic bridge channel; full intrinsic operator response does inside the regulated kernel family.

exact_theorem_style_claims.claim: For every cutoff L>=1 in the regulated static-patch screen family, the offdiag-coupled and dephased kernels agree on screen-visible data but induce different epsilon-recoverable bridge algebras M_N and C^N.
The offdiag-coupled kernel has geometric shrink exp(-distance/(L+1)^2) and cutoff error epsilon_L=1-min_offdiag_shrink.
Full transfer response, off-diagonal relative-entropy response, modular response, and commutator/OTOC-style growth determine the bridge phase inside the regulated family.
Setting the geometric shrink to one recovers the Goal 22 zero-geometry finite transfer family.

remaining_obligations: derive the regulated kernel from an actual static-patch path integral or Hamiltonian
identify a continuum boundary operator dictionary and scaling dimensions
show cutoff errors vanish in a controlled large-L limit
replace finite Type-I algebras with the appropriate Type-II/Type-III limit
derive generalized entropy or QES data from the same dynamics
prove the ER=EPR bridge interpretation in the continuum model

relationship_to_prior_goals.goal21: screen-visible horizon data can hide bridge algebra
relationship_to_prior_goals.goal22: one finite transfer dynamics can derive both screen shadow and bridge channel while screen-restricted data still collide
relationship_to_prior_goals.goal23: the transfer family is replaced by cutoff spherical modes and a geometric kernel with explicit finite-cutoff errors

proof_obligations.claim: Emit the Goal 23 regulated static-patch certificate.
Validate the machine-readable Goal 23 certificate index.

expert_feedback_summary: A regulated finite static-patch testbed with cutoff spherical screen modes and one geometric transfer kernel shows that low-order dS/CFT-like screen data remain insufficient for algebraic ER=EPR connectivity, while full intrinsic operator response recovers the bridge channel with explicit cutoff errors.
limitations: This is finite QEC/OA-QEC mathematics with a regulator. It does not construct a continuum CFT dual, de Sitter quantum gravity, Type-II/Type-III observer algebra, QES formula, or literal ER=EPR-in-dS theorem.
